                                            INTERNET & SAAS (ZIA) : ABOUT SSL/TLS INSPECTION POLICY




Internet & SaaS (ZIA)
About SSL/TLS Inspection Policy
 Watch a video about SSL/TLS Inspection Policy (shows legacy UI).

SSL/TLS Inspection policies are used to perform scanning of the SSL/TLS traffic based on the source
and destination of the traffic.

     The SSL/TLS Inspection policies provide the following benefits and enable you to:
          Simplify the deployment and ongoing operations of SSL/TLS Inspection.
         Address the compliance and operational environment requirements.
          Inspect traffic for malware and data loss.
          Allow, block, or restrict tenants.


Predefined Special Rules
Zscaler provides the following predefined special rules which you can enable or disable based on your
requirements.
These rules are not editable and can only be implemented as is.
     Zscaler-Recommended Exemptions rule: Predefined rule to automatically exempt known
     destinations that cannot be SSL/TLS inspected. This rule is enabled by default.

               The Zscaler-recommended exemptions URL category contains a few dozen
               destinations that cannot be SSL/TLS inspected for various reasons, such as
               certificate pinning. This list also includes Zscaler-owned domains.
               To discover the traffic that is not getting SSL/TLS inspected due to this rule, you can
               search the weblogs using the SSL/TLS Policy Reason field for the reason Not
      
               inspected because of Zscaler best practices. The percentage of traffic that matches
               this rule is commonly very small and less than 1%.
               While it is recommended not to inspect these domains, you can always disable the
               rule with the Edit option. Alternatively, you can leave the rule enabled, but create
               higher order inspect rules if you want to inspect only specific domains outside the
               list.




                                                                            ©2026 Zscaler, Inc. All rights reserved.   1
                                            INTERNET & SAAS (ZIA) : ABOUT SSL/TLS INSPECTION POLICY




    O365 tenant restriction inspection rules: Predefined rules to enable Office 365 Tenancy
    Restrictions for location and remote user traffic.
    O365 One Click rule: Predefined rule, controlled by the Microsoft-Recommended and Legacy
    Office 365 One Click setting.
    UCaaS One Click rule: Predefined rule, controlled by the UCaaS One Click configuration.
    Unauthorized Traffic Bypass for IoT Classifications rule: Predefined rule to allow unauthorized
    traffic bypass SSL/TLS Inspection for IoT classifications in all IoT policy-enabled locations. This
    rule is disabled by default and cannot be deleted. You can modify the Rule Order, Admin Rank,
    Rule Status, Rule Label, and choose Evaluate Other Policies or Bypass Other Policies under Do Not
    Inspect Action for this rule and cannot edit other attributes. You can enable this rule to temporarily
    allow unauthorized traffic to bypass SSL/TLS Inspection, so that Zscaler AI/ML can classify
    devices.

About the SSL/TLS Inspection Policy Page
On the SSL/TLS Inspection Policy page (Policy > Common Configuration > SSL/TLS Inspection >
SSL/TLS Inspection Policy), you can do the following:
  1. View the recommended SSL/TLS Inspection policy.




                                                                            ©2026 Zscaler, Inc. All rights reserved.   2
                                         INTERNET & SAAS (ZIA) : ABOUT SSL/TLS INSPECTION POLICY




2. Search for an SSL/TLS Inspection rule.
3. Select the following column options to view the SSL/TLS Inspection rules accordingly:
       List of columns

              Rule Name
              Rule Action
              Source IP Groups
              URL Categories
              Cloud Applications
              Destination Groups
              Forwarding Gateways
              ZPA Application Segment
              Locations
              Location Groups
              Users
              Groups
              Departments
              Devices
              Device Groups
              Workload Groups
              Remote Users with Kerberos
              Device Trust Level
              CONNECT User-Agent
              Labels
              Description




                                                                        ©2026 Zscaler, Inc. All rights reserved.   3
                                          INTERNET & SAAS (ZIA) : ABOUT SSL/TLS INSPECTION POLICY




4. Add an SSL/TLS Inspection rule.
5. View a list of all SSL/TLS Inspection rules. For each SSL/TLS Inspection rule, you can view:
        Rule Order: The order of the rule.
        Rule Name: The name of the rule.
        Rule Action: The action configured for the rule.
        Labels: The label of the policy rule, if available.
        Description: The description of the policy rule, if available.
6. Arrange the Rule Order or Rule Name column in ascending or descending order.

                                                                          ©2026 Zscaler, Inc. All rights reserved.   4
                                          INTERNET & SAAS (ZIA) : ABOUT SSL/TLS INSPECTION POLICY




7. Edit, delete, or duplicate an SSL/TLS Inspection rule.




                                                                     ©2026 Zscaler, Inc. All rights reserved.   5
